title: Feature Request: Status Icon next to each Connection at Overview
author: Chrisu02
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->

## Expected Behavior
Hello Team,
I would like to Request an Option to see the "Status" LED Icon next to each PC at the Connection Overwiew.

## Current Behavior
Currently, you need to click on each connection to see if a PC is available or not, you can not see quickly which Computers are Online and which not.

## Possible Solution
Adding an Option to show the "Status" LED Between the Connection Icon and Connection Nam...

iterations.description: Feature request for status icons on connections in the overview panel lacks sufficient detail about expected behavior, icon states, and whether it's still relevant on latest builds.
Fix in ad9b9b09 — INCORRECT: commit only changed test-config.json, not source code. Feature was NOT actually implemented.
Bulk verified: commit ad9b9b09 exists (HIGH confidence) — FALSE POSITIVE: commit had no source code changes.

Chrisu02 reported feature not working. Root cause: HostStatusMonitor class existed but was never instantiated. StatusImageList had no reachability overlay. Fix: (1) added ShowHostStatus setting + Options UI toggle, (2) wired HostStatusMonitor to Runtime.LoadConnections, (3) added green/red dot overlay to connection tree icons via StatusImageList. Reply posted on upstream issue.

comments.snippet:
Hi
Which program are you using that gives you this status led behavior?

comments.author: Chrisu02
comments.snippet:
> Hi Which program are you using that gives you this status led behavior?

Unfortunately thats a internally written small tool that someone at the company i work for wrote for ages.
So i don't have the copyright to release this.
The only lucky thing is that it is not compiled, its files are readable code that is run via a script tool, so i can modify it to my need a little bit,  gut i don`t have the source code.
I guess its C# or so.

comments.author: truefriend-cz
comments.snippet: PLEASE DEVS... ADD THIS FEATURE NOW! xD

comments.author: truefriend-cz
comments.snippet:
Hi, thanks for the triage note.

I can retest on the latest upstream nightly/NB build from the official mRemoteNG GitHub Releases. However, I’m not sure which “fork v1.79.x” you’re referring to (could you share a direct link to that fork/release, if it’s different from upstream?).

Once I retest, I’ll report:

- exact build/version tested
- reproducible steps
- expected vs. actual behavior
- logs/screenshots if relevant

Thanks.

A beta build (v1.81.0-beta.4) including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
Hi @Chrisu02, thank you for testing!

You're right — the status icons in the connection tree are not fully wired up yet. The commit was incorrectly marked as implementing this feature. I apologize for the confusion.

**What currently works:**
- In the **Config/Properties panel** (right side), there's a small host status button in the toolbar that checks if a host is reachable when you select a connection.

**What's missing for the full feature you requested:**
- Background monitoring of all conn...

notes: Host status icons in connection tree. HostStatusMonitor existed but was never instantiated. Fixed 2026-03-05: setting + wiring + overlay. Enable via Options > Connections > 'Show host reachability status in connection tree'.
